reasoning: The stock market week generally has a ~52% chance of an upward movement in a neutral scenario. The historical trend model on STLD predicted a 0.54 probability of rising. However, I found out that STLD goes ex-dividend on Tuesday, March 31, 2026. This means the stock price will automatically be adjusted downward by exactly the dividend amount on that day. The dividend is $0.53 per share. Given the current price is around $170.44, a $0.53 downward hit on Tuesday introduces a deterministic headwind. The $0.53 difference represents about -0.31% of the total price. Since normal weekly volatility could easily be 2-3%, this ex-dividend headwind matters but doesn't guarantee a loss. But it meaningfully cuts the probability of finishing higher. Therefore, I adjust the probability downward from 0.52 to 0.41.
